title: begonia × carminata
intro: Begonia × carminata, also known as the firecracker begonia, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Begonia x hybrida and Begonia x corallina. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin America, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Begonia × carminata are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as wounds, burns, and eczema. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and ointments for topical application, or dried and used in capsules or tinctures for internal consumption.
